title: Loxogramme lankokiensis
taxonomy: Loxogramme lankokiensis is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. It belongs to the genus Loxogramme, characterized by their distinctive leaf shapes. The species Lankokiensis is a species of fern that is native to Sri Lanka. Loxogramme lankokiensis is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and the division Pteridophyta. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics.
benefits: Loxogramme lankokiensis is known for its potential health benefits, including antioxidant properties that may help protect against cell damage and inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its extracts have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which could contribute to its potential therapeutic applications. Loxogramme lankokiensis may also have cardiovascular benefits, including lowering blood pressure and improving lipid profiles. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's health benefits and confirm its efficacy in treating specific health conditions.

preparations: Loxogramme lankokiensis is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine, and herbal preparations derived from it are believed to have various health benefits.
